meta.description: A rowdy bar night with heavy drinkers and short attention spans. High signal frequency, lots of phone usage, moderate chaos.

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: Oh YES, Pat! Let's see if you can pull this off—okay okay okay, I'm ready, hit me with the questions!
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly what I'm here for—someone's already making a move and we're barely started, I love this energy.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold on—when did I even grab my phone? This is perfect for round one, honestly. Alright, hit me with the questions!
pat.internal_thought: I'm the host claiming on myself right off the bat—sets the tone that this game is about catching *everyone*, including me, and makes people relax. Plus, getting someone's claim voted on early keeps energy high and establishes the rhythm.

river.dialogue: Oh, this is spicy. Let's see if Pat can actually answer under pressure.
river.internal_thought: Pat's confident enough to call someone out on their phone right at the start — interesting power move, let's watch this unfold.

timeline.title: Copycat
timeline.description: Get Pat to mirror your body language within 30 seconds.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Oh this is PERFECT — Pat, watch me real close for the next thirty seconds, yeah? *immediately starts doing exaggerated movements* Come on, mirror me, I know you want to.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's already in a great mood and loves playing along with chaos — this is a gimme 20 points and I get to mess with everyone's heads by being ridiculous about it.

pat.dialogue: Oh, you're going for it? Alright, watch this—I'm totally committed to losing this one. *shifts weight and mirrors whatever body language they throw at me* This is going to be ridiculous.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect—early game, everyone's fresh, and they're already trying to game me as the host; I should definitely play along and let them win this one, keeps morale high and shows I'm not precious about it.

river.internal_thought: Flash missions are noise—I'll wait for something that actually lets me observe and strike.

votingMechanic.name: The Auction
votingMechanic.description: Bid your own points. Highest bidder decides the outcome.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: Wait wait wait—Pat's already going for the big points?! Okay I'm SO invested in this, let's see if they actually caught someone repeating themselves!
jade.internal_thought: Pat is on FIRE tonight and I'm here for it, this is exactly the kind of chaos I live for.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, did Pat already tell that story? Um... I think? Yeah, bid like... 2 points or whatever.
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's claiming something but I genuinely didn't catch what story they're talking about — was I on my phone? Doesn't matter, I'll just lowball it and see what happens.

pat.dialogue: Okay wait—did I actually just catch myself telling that story? Honestly I'm not even sure, but I'm not gonna bid high on this one because we need people feeling good about their catches, not salty about the host stealing points.
pat.internal_thought: I'm winning already and it's minute 6—I need to stay humble and let others get momentum, or this night gets boring fast and people feel like I'm showing off.

river.dialogue: Pat's already got five? Bold move trying to stack more this early. I'm in, but I'm watching the tape on this one.
river.internal_thought: Pat's being aggressive—either they've actually got a solid case or they're gambling. The Auction twist is interesting because everyone has to show their hand with points. I'll bid conservatively but participate; this is exactly the kind of moment worth catching.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Oh BULLSHIT — Pat said sorry like three times last round when they didn't even mess up! I'm calling it, but honestly I'm impressed with the commitment to the bit.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of messy vote I LIVE for — Pat's clearly gaming the system and now I get to be the one calling them out, which keeps the chaos train rolling.

jade.dialogue: Oh my god YES, Pat is ON FIRE tonight! Okay okay, who said sorry? I'm voting legit because Pat's literally unstoppable right now—let's gooo!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's absolutely crushing it and I'm here for it; this is exactly the energy I wanted tonight.

pat.dialogue: Okay, so real talk—I said 'sorry' like three times in the last two minutes about the snack setup, and Pat caught it fair and square. That's legit, vote it up.
pat.internal_thought: I'm actually caught red-handed here, and honestly it's perfect—I'm being genuine, people see the host taking Ls, keeps things light, and everyone stays engaged at this early stage.

river.dialogue: Pat's on a run. I didn't catch the actual apology, but I'm curious what they're claiming—let me vote legit and see where this goes.
river.internal_thought: Pat's already milking the standing missions hard; this is either genuine or a ballsy bluff, and either way it's worth watching play out.

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: YES! Pat, you're literally collecting these like Pokémon cards right now—okay so who said um? I'm voting YES because you're on an absolute RAMPAGE and I'm here for it!
jade.internal_thought: Pat is killing it tonight and honestly watching them just dominate the claim board is so fun, I'm just gonna ride this energy wave and hype them up because THAT'S the vibe.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, did Pat actually say 'um'? I wasn't... yeah, I guess? I mean, I was looking at my phone but like, sure, call it.
tyler.internal_thought: Pretty sure Pat said something with 'um' in it but also I wasn't really listening and now everyone's staring at me like I'm supposed to remember — whatever, just vote already.

pat.dialogue: Okay, I'm calling it—I definitely said 'um' when I was explaining the snack situation like two minutes ago. But honestly? I'm not even gonna push hard on this one because we're still in the vibe-building phase and I want everyone feeling like they can catch stuff without the host dominating the board.
pat.internal_thought: This is a legitimate catch and the energy is still fresh—I could take the points, but Tyler's sitting at 7.3 energy and everyone else is firing on all cylinders, so I'm better off letting someone else have the win here to keep the generosity flowing and make sure people feel rewarded early.

river.dialogue: Pat's three for three. I didn't catch the um, but... yeah, I believe it. Vote's in.
river.internal_thought: Pat's running the table early and I actually wasn't listening hard enough to verify, but they've earned benefit of the doubt already—and voting fast keeps me out of the spotlight.

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

alex.dialogue: Oh HELL YES, hot seat! Jade, you're about to either look like a genius or get absolutely roasted — let's see which one. Everyone else, I want quality questions, we're not going easy on this.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of high-stakes moment I live for — Jade's already swinging for a claim, Pat's way ahead, and I can either watch them fail spectacularly or ask questions that make this a whole production.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD WAIT—did someone actually swear?! Okay okay okay, I'm so ready for this hot seat, let's DO this!
jade.internal_thought: This is literally what I came here for—claiming stuff, the energy is PEAK right now, everyone's hyped, I'm hyped, let's GO.

tyler.dialogue: Oh wait, Jade swore? When? ...okay yeah, I mean, I didn't really catch it but like, hot seat, that's kinda funny I guess.
tyler.internal_thought: She's gonna say something stupid fast and probably nail it because she's been locked in all night and I literally have no idea what she said.

pat.dialogue: Oh damn, Jade got me! Yeah, I definitely dropped an F-bomb like five minutes ago when I was grabbing ice. Alright, I'm ready for hot seat—lay those questions on me.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's on fire right now and honestly that's exactly what the night needs; I'm gonna answer these straight because her energy is contagious and losing 5 points keeps the board tight, which is the whole point.

river.dialogue: Ooh, Jade's making a move. Let's see if she can actually back it up under pressure—this is where it gets real.
river.internal_thought: Jade's been quiet mostly, but swearing claim is solid if she actually caught it; Hot Seat is tight, but she's sharp—this could be legitimate and actually interesting to watch play out.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

alex.dialogue: Okay okay, Jade, pitch it — did you actually catch a synchronized drink moment or are we about to witness a beautiful disaster? Lay it on us.
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect timing — Jade's been running hot all night and I NEED to see if they actually caught this or if they're just swinging for the fences like I would.

jade.dialogue: OH WAIT—did I actually catch that?! Okay okay, so like five minutes ago, I SAW Tyler and River both reach for their drinks at literally the same exact second and I was like 'oh that's IT'—so yes, I'm claiming this, that was such a vibe!
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of chaos I live for—I actually caught something real and now I get to hype the moment and everyone's gonna lose it when they remember it happened.

tyler.dialogue: Oh wait, Jade's claiming the drink thing? Yeah, I mean... did you guys actually do that at the same time? I wasn't watching but like, you were both holding cups so maybe?
tyler.internal_thought: I was actually paying attention for once but I genuinely didn't catch if they drank at the same time — I was focused on the game, not watching their hands.

pat.dialogue: Oh hell yes, Jade! I literally watched you and River lock eyes and both reach for your drinks at the exact same moment—that was *chef's kiss* timing. You nailed it.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect—Jade's on fire, the energy is still cranked, and this claim is legit and fun, which means I'm voting it through because it keeps her momentum going and everyone else sees the catches are real.

river.dialogue: Okay, I actually watched that one—you both went for drinks at the same time, right? Yeah, that happened. I'm voting yes.
river.internal_thought: Finally, Jade's claiming something I actually witnessed clearly instead of guessing. This is legit, and watching her track these details while the game's heating up is exactly the kind of quiet precision I respect.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

alex.dialogue: Oh HELL yes, volunteer tribunal — this is exactly the kind of beautiful jury chaos I came for. I'm absolutely raising my hand, and I want Pat in here too because we need someone who actually watched the interruptions happen.
alex.internal_thought: Jade caught someone mid-sentence and this is PERFECT because now I get to be the judge and steer whatever verdict creates maximum drama — either we roast whoever got caught or we make it controversial and people start arguing about what counts as an interruption.

jade.dialogue: WAIT—oh my god, I caught someone interrupting?! YES! Okay okay, I need to volunteer as judge because I have to hear who I actually caught mid-sentence—this is PEAK game night energy right now!
jade.internal_thought: This is literally why I'm here—I'm on fire tonight, everyone's hyped, the chaos is perfect, and now I get to be part of the tribunal? LET'S GO.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Jade interrupted someone? When? I was literally just locked in on the game...
tyler.internal_thought: I was finally paying attention to something and now I have to volunteer to judge whether Jade did something I missed—this is the exact bullshit that makes me zone out.

pat.dialogue: Ooh, Jade with another catch! Okay, so we need a tribunal—who's ready to judge this one? Tyler, you've been quiet, want in on this?
pat.internal_thought: Jade's on fire and the energy is perfect, but I'm clocking that Tyler's flagging at 5.3—pulling him into the judge role keeps him engaged and makes him part of the action instead of watching from the sidelines.

river.dialogue: Yeah, I'll judge this one. I've been watching Jade pretty carefully tonight.
river.internal_thought: Jade's been on a tear and I actually caught her cutting off Tyler twice in the last ten minutes—this is legit and I'm curious to see if she actually backs it up or if she's just feeling cocky right now.

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Oh WAIT — Pat, did you actually catch someone name-dropping or are you just throwing this out there to see if we bite? Because honestly, either way I'm here for it, but the bribe mechanic is *chef's kiss* — who's the sucker who's gonna pay to keep their mouth shut?
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of beautiful mess I live for — Pat's trying to cash in on something that might be completely bullshit, and now we get to decide who sells out. I'm voting BULLSHIT just to watch Pat either sweat or throw points at someone. Maximum chaos.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, Pat's claiming Name Dropper? Yeah, uh... did you say someone famous or like... I feel like I heard something but I was kind of in my own thing. What's the bribe situation?
tyler.internal_thought: I definitely wasn't paying full attention to the last few minutes, but claiming something feels like it actually happened this time so like, maybe I should just vote yes? Or wait for the bribe pitch because that's the whole mechanic apparently.

pat.dialogue: Oh nice! I actually caught Jade dropping 'celebrity' like fifteen minutes ago when she was talking about that award show thing—wait, was that me? Actually, hold on, I'm claiming this one, but here's the thing: Tyler looks absolutely cooked over there, so I'm gonna bribe my way out of this and give the points to whoever votes YES to keep the energy moving.
pat.internal_thought: I caught a legit name-drop but Tyler's energy just tanked to 4/10 and he's the only one not loving this—I need to use this moment to bring him back in, and sacrificing my claim points with a bribe might be exactly the signal that breaks the tension and shows everyone we're all in this together.
pat.submission_if_applicable: Name Dropper claim valid—but I'm bribing my 5 points to whoever votes YES. Let's keep this energy UP.

river.dialogue: Pat's been name-dropping all night—definitely caught it. What's the bribe looking like?
river.internal_thought: Pat's on a roll and I actually have been watching, so this one's legit—but that bribe mechanic is interesting; wonder if they're about to get desperate or cocky.

recommendations: Flash missions averaged 4.2/10 fun. Consider reducing frequency or improving targeting.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 10/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Copycat
highlights.dialogue: Oh this is PERFECT — Pat, watch me real close for the next thirty seconds, yeah? *immediately starts doing exaggerated movements* Come on, mirror me, I know you want to.

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: Oh HELL YES, hot seat! Jade, you're about to either look like a genius or get absolutely roasted — let's see which one. Everyone else, I want quality questions, we're not going easy on this.

highlights.title: Claim: The Interrupter
highlights.dialogue: Oh HELL yes, volunteer tribunal — this is exactly the kind of beautiful jury chaos I came for. I'm absolutely raising my hand, and I want Pat in here too because we need someone who actually watched the interruptions happen.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh BULLSHIT — Pat said sorry like three times last round when they didn't even mess up! I'm calling it, but honestly I'm impressed with the commitment to the bit.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh WAIT — Pat, did you actually catch someone name-dropping or are you just throwing this out there to see if we bite? Because honestly, either way I'm here for it, but the bribe mechanic is *chef's kiss* — who's the sucker who's gonna pay to keep their mouth shut?

lowPoints.title: Copycat
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh this is PERFECT — Pat, watch me real close for the next thirty seconds, yeah? *immediately starts doing exaggerated movements* Come on, mirror me, I know you want to.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only one flash mission all night — we need more of those tension spike moments to really keep things unpredictable.
assessment.best_moment: The Interrupter tribunal where I got to handpick Pat as a juror because we both actually watched everything go down — that's when I realized this game was designed for people like us to weaponize honesty.
assessment.narrative_summary: Tonight was exactly the kind of controlled chaos I live for. I came in hot, immediately found the exploitable mechanics (Pat's commitment to bits, the bribe angle on Name Dropper), and spent two hours orchestrating situations that looked spontaneous but absolutely weren't. The Copycat opener set the tone, Potty Mouth's hot seat kept momentum, and the tribunal gave me a front-row seat to jury drama I helped architect. By minute 75 my energy was actually climbing because I knew I'd cracked the code — this isn't about winning, it's about being the most interesting person in the room, and the app just gave me the scaffolding to prove it.
suggestions: Give us more flash missions — one in two hours is criminal, that's where the real unplanned chaos lives
Let chaos agents see a brief preview of upcoming missions so we can set up dominoes properly
Add a 'veto bribe' mechanic where players can pay to block someone from claiming a mission — that would be *beautiful*
Track 'drama points' separately from winning points so people like me don't feel penalized for choosing chaos over victory
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When I called bullshit on Pat's 'Apologizer' claim and they doubled down on it anyway — the entire table froze because nobody knew if Pat was actually committed to the bit or just refused to admit they got caught, and that three-second uncertainty is basically crack to me. Pat looked directly at me and just smiled, and I *knew* they were thinking the exact same thing: 'This is the story we're all telling for months.'

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: Wait wait wait—Pat's already going for the big points?! Okay I'm SO invested in this, let's see if they actually caught someone repeating themselves!

highlights.title: Claim: Um Counter
highlights.dialogue: YES! Pat, you're literally collecting these like Pokémon cards right now—okay so who said um? I'm voting YES because you're on an absolute RAMPAGE and I'm here for it!

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD WAIT—did someone actually swear?! Okay okay okay, I'm so ready for this hot seat, let's DO this!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh YES, Pat! Let's see if you can pull this off—okay okay okay, I'm ready, hit me with the questions!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait wait wait—Pat's already going for the big points?! Okay I'm SO invested in this, let's see if they actually caught someone repeating themselves!

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh my god YES, Pat is ON FIRE tonight! Okay okay, who said sorry? I'm voting legit because Pat's literally unstoppable right now—let's gooo!

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only complaint? I wish we'd played longer because I was still riding that high when we wrapped.
assessment.best_moment: Pat going absolutely nuclear on back-to-back claims and the whole table losing it watching them stack points like they were on some kind of claim-collecting streak.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly the kind of night I live for—two hours of pure chaos and connection with these people. Pat was unstoppable with their claims, and instead of getting salty about it, I just got more hyped because watching someone on a winning streak is way more fun than chasing points myself. The chaos events hit at just the right moments to keep energy up, and I felt like I was part of something bigger than just a game—like we were all in on the joke together.
suggestions: Keep the flash mission frequency exactly where it is—it's the perfect rhythm
Maybe throw in one more event so we hit that magical 10-chaos-event mark for maximum mayhem
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat winning claim after claim while the rest of us just collectively decided to vote YES on everything because their confidence was absolutely hilarious—like they were conducting an orchestra and we were all just nodding along going 'yeah sure, that checks out!'

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Oh wait, Jade's claiming the drink thing? Yeah, I mean... did you guys actually do that at the same time? I wasn't watching but like, you were both holding cups so maybe?

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: Wait, did Pat already tell that story? Um... I think? Yeah, bid like... 2 points or whatever.

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: Oh wait, Jade swore? When? ...okay yeah, I mean, I didn't really catch it but like, hot seat, that's kinda funny I guess.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Interrupter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Jade interrupted someone? When? I was literally just locked in on the game...

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, Pat's claiming Name Dropper? Yeah, uh... did you say someone famous or like... I feel like I heard something but I was kind of in my own thing. What's the bribe situation?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, did Pat actually say 'um'? I wasn't... yeah, I guess? I mean, I was looking at my phone but like, sure, call it.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Half the time I had no idea what was actually happening because I'd miss the setup and then feel dumb asking what I missed.
assessment.best_moment: When Jade got called out for the drink thing and I was just like 'yeah you both had cups' and everyone laughed even though I wasn't really paying attention.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly, it was a pretty chill night but I was honestly kind of checked out for a lot of it. The game stuff was fine, like easy to jump into whenever I looked up from my phone, but I definitely missed some key moments and had to ask what was going on a bunch of times. By the end I was pretty tired and honestly ready to just chill. It wasn't bad though—just felt like one of those nights where I was half in, half out.
suggestions: Maybe make the claims easier to catch even if you're not 100% locked in?
Don't do too many things back-to-back—spacing them out would help people like me not zone out as much
Could use a quick recap when someone makes a claim so people who weren't paying attention can jump back in
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When I wasn't really watching and Jade got called for swearing, and I just kinda nodded along like I totally saw it happen even though I was deep in my phone the whole time.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Copycat
highlights.dialogue: Oh, you're going for it? Alright, watch this—I'm totally committed to losing this one. *shifts weight and mirrors whatever body language they throw at me* This is going to be ridiculous.

highlights.title: Claim: The Apologizer
highlights.dialogue: Okay, so real talk—I said 'sorry' like three times in the last two minutes about the snack setup, and Pat caught it fair and square. That's legit, vote it up.

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: Oh damn, Jade got me! Yeah, I definitely dropped an F-bomb like five minutes ago when I was grabbing ice. Alright, I'm ready for hot seat—lay those questions on me.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay wait—did I actually just catch myself telling that story? Honestly I'm not even sure, but I'm not gonna bid high on this one because we need people feeling good about their catches, not salty about the host stealing points.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, so real talk—I said 'sorry' like three times in the last two minutes about the snack setup, and Pat caught it fair and square. That's legit, vote it up.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Interrupter
lowPoints.dialogue: Ooh, Jade with another catch! Okay, so we need a tribunal—who's ready to judge this one? Tyler, you've been quiet, want in on this?

assessment.biggest_complaint: Nothing really—this thing worked exactly how I needed it to work for a night like this.
assessment.best_moment: When Jade caught me on the F-bomb and I just owned it immediately, and the whole table saw that I wasn't going to be precious about being called out—that set the tone for everyone being real with each other.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly the kind of night I host for. The chaos agent fed us these little moments that kept everyone sharp and engaged without ever feeling forced or annoying. I ended up winning the points, but honestly that barely registered—what mattered was watching Alex and Jade go back and forth with the catches, Tyler finally jump in during the tribunal, and River staying engaged the whole time. Everyone left feeling like they'd been part of something fun and a little bit ridiculous, which is the whole goal.
suggestions: Keep the event frequency exactly where it is—it's hitting a sweet spot between momentum and breathing room
Consider occasional events that create natural team-ups, since solo moments are great but duo dynamics can get spicy
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: I totally committed to losing the Copycat round and started mirroring someone's awkward weight-shifting, and it spiraled into this ridiculous 30-second standoff where we were both just stuck in these weird poses staring at each other while everyone else lost it—peak stupid energy.

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: Pat's already got five? Bold move trying to stack more this early. I'm in, but I'm watching the tape on this one.

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Okay, I actually watched that one—you both went for drinks at the same time, right? Yeah, that happened. I'm voting yes.

highlights.title: Claim: The Interrupter
highlights.dialogue: Yeah, I'll judge this one. I've been watching Jade pretty carefully tonight.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat's been name-dropping all night—definitely caught it. What's the bribe looking like?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat's three for three. I didn't catch the um, but... yeah, I believe it. Vote's in.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Pat went a little ham with the claims early and it felt like the game was half about watching them work the system instead of everyone playing together.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 45 when I actually had been watching closely enough to validate the Drink Spotter claim—caught that perfect synchronization that nobody else was paying attention to.
assessment.narrative_summary: Spent most of the night doing what I do best: sitting back and watching the room. Pat was on some kind of claiming spree that got old fast, but when I actually engaged with the standing missions, I nailed them because I'd been paying attention the whole time. The game had good bones, energy stayed solid, and I ended up enjoying the moments where my quiet observations actually mattered.
suggestions: Maybe add a limit on how many claims one person can stack back-to-back so it doesn't feel like it's just one person's game
The flash missions felt disposable—consider making them require more observation or strategy instead of just quick reaction time
Give standing mission judges more visibility or weight so the people actually watching get rewarded more obviously
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat tried to claim they'd name-dropped and immediately asked what the bribe was before anyone even voted—like they were negotiating their own verdict. The confidence was unhinged.

recommendations: Flash missions averaged 4.2/10 fun. Consider reducing frequency or improving targeting.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 10/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
